acceptanceCriteria.statement: The program identifies reviewed Skills as the first low-burden terminal MVP inside a broader resource-governance funnel.
The program does not claim that this repository owns future non-Skill terminal releases or upstream resource governance.
Third-party Skill payload requires pinned provenance, license, security, portability, overlap, adaptation, validation, and approval evidence before release.
Official, runtime-owned, built-in, plugin-owned, and first-party Skill bodies remain environment-owned baselines rather than curated inventory.
Approved release content and abstract capability topology remain agent-neutral, with product-specific integration kept outside curated payload authority.
Every supported consumer Agent has dated evidence for instruction discovery, Skill locations and ownership, precedence, projection, verification, backup, and restore behavior.
The program defines a smallest-sufficient layered path from instructions through Skills, deterministic mechanisms, project standards, evidence, and human authority.
Conflicting generic Skill guidance defers to an admitted project-owned hard standard, while standard admission remains outside this repository.
A reusable standard candidate package contract preserves source lineage, repeated evidence, scope, counterexamples, verification, versioning, migration, and authority boundaries.

Cross-project research and standard candidates do not remain solely in consumer configuration repositories; bounded packages target YIYUAN-CALIBRATION for durable calibration custody before any YIYUAN-ASSETS admission decision.

This repository does not itself admit or publish CALIBRATION or ASSETS standards; cross-repository delivery requires separate authority, and YIYUAN-ASSETS retains project hard-standard admission authority.
Consumer, community, security, license, upstream-refresh, and validation feedback can return released content to review and revision.
The lifecycle supports update, supersession, deprecation, migration, rollback, and retirement without optimizing for inventory growth.

verifications.method: Compare the machine program authority with the English and Chinese public projections and the continuous harness model.
verifications.expectedResult: The first-terminal MVP, upstream boundary, future-terminal boundary, and non-ownership claims agree.
verifications.evidenceRequirement: Current checked-in program, harness, and README paths.

verifications.method: Run the deterministic repository verifier and inspect the approved manifest and admission evidence.
verifications.expectedResult: Only approved, provenance-complete third-party payload is released and official/runtime bodies remain excluded.
verifications.evidenceRequirement: Passing command output plus checked-in admission and manifest records.

verifications.method: Run repository verification, topology checks, and release-manifest checks against agent-neutral contracts.
verifications.expectedResult: Approved payload and abstract topology do not claim consumer-specific runtime ownership.
verifications.evidenceRequirement: Passing deterministic checks and reviewed public boundary text.

verifications.method: For each consumer, compare official documentation, local read-only configuration inventory, and minimal behavior probes under a separately authorized consumer-side task.
verifications.expectedResult: Every claimed consumer mapping has dated evidence and unresolved consumers remain partial rather than inferred.
verifications.evidenceRequirement: Consumer-owned dated mapping reports; current repository evidence alone is insufficient.

verifications.method: Check that program and harness projections define carrier roles, authority order, conflict precedence, and no-text-only reliability claim.
verifications.expectedResult: Text, Skill, deterministic mechanism, project standard, evidence, and human authority roles are distinct and minimally sufficient.
verifications.evidenceRequirement: Current program and harness projections; implementation coverage remains separately assessed.

verifications.method: Validate a future standard candidate schema and at least one evidence-backed fixture without performing an unauthorized cross-repository write.
verifications.expectedResult: Candidate packages preserve lineage, declare YIYUAN-CALIBRATION as their calibration custody target, and remain non-authoritative until project admission.
verifications.evidenceRequirement: A checked-in contract, fixtures, deterministic tests, an explicit calibration handoff target, and an external authority boundary.

verifications.method: Inspect the machine-readable delivery fields and public projections for consumer configuration role, calibration custody target, and project admission authority.
verifications.expectedResult: Consumer configuration repositories are evidence and feedback surfaces, YIYUAN-CALIBRATION is the durable calibration custody target, and YIYUAN-ASSETS owns project admission decisions.
verifications.evidenceRequirement: Current program plan, harness model, and English and Chinese README projections.

verifications.method: Inspect the program authority and repository write boundaries.
verifications.expectedResult: No calibration or product standard path is owned, written, or claimed as admitted by this repository.
verifications.evidenceRequirement: Machine-readable non-goals and public boundary documentation.

verifications.method: Inspect lifecycle policy, harness feedback inputs, and future round evidence for update, deprecation, migration, rollback, and retirement decisions.
verifications.expectedResult: Lifecycle outcomes are evidence-backed and do not accumulate indefinitely by default.
verifications.evidenceRequirement: Current policy and harness plus future decision records for implemented metabolism paths.
